title: fagraea coromandelina
intro: Fagraea coromandelina, also known as the fragrant frangipani or Indian laurel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Fagraea coromandelina include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
